data.title: Amazon to invest $233M to strengthen India operations network
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) plans to invest about $233M in its India business to support network expansion and upgrades for faster and more reliable deliveries.

“This investment will enhance processing capacity, improve fulfilment speed, and increase efficiency across the company's operations network that will help Amazon serve customers across India faster and more reliably,” the company said in a release.

As part of the investment, the company will continue work on some initiatives to support its contractors and employees, including rest spots for delivery personnel; financial education and support; scholarships for associates’ children; and healthcare support for truck drivers.

The e-commerce giant also said it is developing new software and tools to help delivery personnel with navigation, safety, and the overall delivery process.

data.title: Canada signals higher duties on U.S. steel if a trade deal is not reached
data.description:
Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said that Canada will revise the current 25% retaliatory tariffs on U.S. steel and aluminum if no new trade agreement is reached within 30 days.

The exact tariffs rate will be adjusted based on the outcome of talks between the leaders. Carney emphasized that Canada will not immediately match the recent U.S. tariff hikes—which saw steel and aluminum duties rise from 25% to 50% earlier this month.

"Canada will adjust its existing counter-tariffs on U.S. steel and aluminum products on July 21 to levels consistent with progress made in the broader trading agreement with the United States," Carney told a press conference.

Carney also said he's establishing a new "tariff rate quota," to stabilize the domestic market and prevent harmful trade diversion. "We must reinforce our strength at home and safeguard Canadian workers and businesses from the unjust U.S. tariffs that exist at present," Carney added.

Here is a list of ETFs to track the broader U.S. market: (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

And here are ETFs to track Canadian market: (BATS:BBCA), (NYSEARCA:EWC), (NYSEARCA:FLCA), (NYSEARCA:FXC).

data.title: Artemis Gold appoints Dale Andres as CEO and Director
Meta plans to hire Safe Superintelligence CEO Daniel Gross and Nat Friedman to push AI - report
FinVolution unveils proposed $130 million convertible senior notes offering
data.description:
• FinVolution Group (NYSE:FINV) on Thursday announced a proposed offering of convertible senior notes in an aggregate principal amount of $130 million due 2030.
  • The company intends to grant the initial purchasers an option to purchase up to an additional $20 million in aggregate principal amount of the notes.

  • FinVolution Group (NYSE:FINV) plans to use the net proceeds for international business expansions, replenishment of working capital, and funding the concurrent repurchase.

  • When issued, the Notes will be senior, unsecured obligations of the company. The Notes will mature on July 1, 2030, unless repurchased, redeemed, or converted in accordance with their terms before such date.

data.title: Belo Sun Mining announces Ayesha Hira to step down as interim President & CEO
NTT gains support for $16.3 billion takeover of AI unit
data.description:
Nippon Telegraph and Telephone announced Friday that it has secured sufficient bids to acquire NTT Data Group (OTCPK:NTTDF) in a ¥2.37 trillion ($16.3 billion) deal aimed at advancing its AI ambitions and streamlining its corporate structure.

The Japanese company will start the settlement on June 26, it said in a statement.

NTT, which owns about 58% of NTT Data, had last month offered ¥4,000 for every share it doesn’t already own in the company with plans to take it private.

Roughly one-third government-owned, NTT is competing with KDDI Corp (OTCPK:KDDIY). and SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) (OTCPK:SFTBF) in the accelerating AI race, as Tokyo urges domestic firms to build a homegrown AI platform to rival global players like Microsoft-backed OpenAI (MSFT) and China’s DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK).

data.title: UK retail sales drop more than expected in May
data.description:
• Retail sales in the UK dropped 2.70% in May over the previous month, a bigger fall compared to consensus estimates of a 0.5% decline.

  • Year-over-year, the figure declined 1.3%.

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB)
  • Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: Crude oil tanker company DHT Holdings to acquire a 2018 built VLCC
data.description:
• Crude oil tanker company DHT Holdings (NYSE:DHT) has agreed to acquire a VLCC built in 2018 at Hyundai Heavy Industries (HHI), for $107 million, it said on Thursday.
  • The vessel is scheduled to be delivered towards the end of the third quarter of 2025.
  • The acquisition will be financed through the company’s available liquidity and projected mortgage debt.
  • The vessel was built to a high specification by its current owner and is fitted with an exhaust gas cleaning system.
  • The acquisition will improve DHT’s age profile and will further improve the DHT fleet’s efficiency metrics.

data.title: SoftBank's Masayoshi Son is said to propose $1 trillion U.S. AI hub to TSMC and Trump team
data.description:
SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) (OTCPK:SFTBF) founder Masayoshi Son is looking to team up with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM) to realize what could be his biggest bet yet — a trillion-dollar industrial complex in Arizona to build robots and artificial intelligence, Bloomberg reported Friday.

Son envisions a version of the vast manufacturing hub of China’s Shenzhen that would bring back high-tech manufacturing to the US, the report said.

The park may comprise production lines for AI-powered industrial robots, as the Japanese tech investment group shifts focus to long-term growth opportunities.

The company is making a bold push into artificial intelligence, pledging up to $40 billion in investments toward OpenAI—starting with an initial $8.5 billion through its Vision Fund 2.

SoftBank Group (OTCPK:SFTBY) (OTCPK:SFTBF) raised $4.8 billion by selling its stake in T-Mobile US (TMUS) to fund AI investments.

US tech giants Meta (META), Amazon (AMZN), Alphabet (GOOG), and Microsoft (MSFT), meanwhile, are set to spend a record $320 billion on AI and data centers in 2025, up from $230 billion in 2024.
